app.title: Sunday Pádel

errors.not_a_team_member: You are not on this team.

errors.not_a_member: You are not a member of this group anymore.
errors.group_required: Open the app from a group's pinned message.
errors.already_in_team: You are already in a team.
errors.partner_already_in_team: That player is already in a team.
errors.partner_not_registered: That player isn't registered to play.
errors.partner_is_self: Pick someone else as a partner.
errors.invalid_set_count: A match must have 2 or 3 sets.

tournament.none: No tournament is open right now.
tournament.endConfirm: End this tournament? No more matches can be submitted.

teams.title: Team
teams.nooneLooking: No one else is looking for a partner yet.
teams.leaveConfirm: Leave the team ({{name}})? Both of you will go back to the looking-for-teammate list.

live.notInTeam: You're not on a team — you can spectate.

admin.deleteConfirm: Delete this match ({{score}})? This cannot be undone.

help.title: Help
help.footer: Tap the back arrow or Close to return.

intro.title: What is this?
intro.body: Sunday Pádel is a tiny tournament tracker for casual groups. Each Telegram group runs its own tournaments. The app remembers who is playing, who paired with whom, every match and the running leaderboard.

registration.title: Registration
registration.body: On the Current tab, toggle ‘Playing’ to add yourself to the next tournament. Toggle ‘BBQ’ if you’ll stay for food. You can change either at any time while registration is open.

teams.title: Pairing up
teams.body: Once you’re marked Playing, you’ll see everyone else who’s looking for a partner. Tap ‘Pair up’ and confirm — the team is formed instantly. The previous partner you played with is shown first with a star. Use ‘Leave team’ if you change your mind before the tournament starts.

live.title: During a live tournament
live.body: When the admin starts the tournament, the Submit-a-match form appears. Pick the opposing team, type each set’s score, then tap the Telegram main button (or Submit). At least one valid set is required.

matches.title: Confirm and dispute
matches.body: The opposing team should Confirm your submission. If a score is wrong, anyone in the match can Dispute it and the admin can fix or delete the match. Submissions auto-confirm after 30 minutes if no one acts.

leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
leaderboard.body: Teams need a few matches to be ranked (your group’s admin sets the minimum). Ranking uses win rate, then set ratio, then game ratio.

history.title: History tab
history.body: Past tournaments show the podium (top 3) for each event so you can see who won what and when.

overall.title: Overall scores
overall.body: Each finished tournament awards podium points (10 / 7 / 5 / 3 / 1) plus a small bonus for every win. The Overall tab is your group’s all-time leaderboard.

admin.title: Admin tools
admin.body: Group admins can create tournaments, start them (which closes registration and forms the brackets), end them (which freezes the leaderboard and awards points), edit or delete results, mark a disputed match confirmed, and export CSVs of registrations and results.

language.title: Language
language.body: Pick your language from the dropdown at the top — your choice is remembered for this Telegram account across devices.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body: The app only stores what’s needed to run tournaments: your Telegram name, your registrations and the matches you played. Nothing is shared outside your group.

overall.empty: No overall scores yet — end a tournament to start the ranking.
